@edrosa3485 That's probably why he dropped it. GW changed 40k Cannon just to appease the DEI overlords at Amazon because they have been trying to get a major studio to lock in for decades now. Amazon says, "we need to have female Custodes", all of a sudden GW is like, "There have ALWAYS been female custodes and if you disagree, you don't have a place in our fandom". I assume Cavill dropped the series because the amount of changes, DEI nonsense and wokeness that GW would have caved to and made to the 40k universe in order to get the project going. 

I could just imagine being him, a big 40k fan, in a production meeting with toxic DEI/ESG filled corporate boards and them saying, "We should make at least half of the space marines women", and him saying, "Space Marines and Custodes are all men, women have their own representation in different factions". Then they just say, "That is nice for the game, but we need the to have at least (abc) character and (xyz) character to be female and (123) gender identity/sexuality". Then he would probably just say, "They literally are all men for a reason and gender or gender identity are not even parts of the story". They say, "Well we still need this to happen". After months of not agreeing, GW sees red flags and sees their cash grab going downhill and says, "Look, there ARE female Custodes". Cavill just quits before anymore damage is done.

replies.author: @jjc5407
replies.text:
@ sure, any of the EU stories could be made into animations... but that's not where the money would be for Star Wars.  Live action was always going to be at the forefront.

It's just a shame Disney didn't do the sensible thing and start a new saga set hundreds of years after George's.  They could have left the EU completely alone and told a whole new tale.  Some old characters could have appeared in cameos via holocrons or as spirits.  Galactic technology would have advanced in line with film production technology.

For those that aren't familiar with the EU Anakin fulfilling his destiny as the Chosen One by destroying the Sith and restoring balance to the Force would have actually had a lasting effect.  All it needed was for the audience to know that Luke had fulfilled his mission to restore the Jedi Order and peace had reigned again for a long time, but the Dark Side will never be entirely defeated and was about to rise again.

replies.author: @jjc5407
replies.text:
@ is that the one with Cade Skywalker?  If so then no, it was only set 130 years later, which was the furthest into the future after the movies the EU went.

They should just have left the entire EU as it was and not even referenced it.  Those who love it could have kept it as canon and those who've never engaged with it wouldn't have had to.

Personally having read all the EU books (novels and young adult), a lot of the comics and numerous short stories I found it a very mixed bag, which isn't surprising given how many different writers were involved.  Troy Denning was by far the worst.  I can't stand his contributions.

replies.author: @John-fk2ky
replies.text: @jjc5407 I have not read anything by Denning, but I can’t imagine it being worse than Karen Travis. She was probably the original entitled “creative”.

replies.author: @jjc5407
replies.text:
@ they were 2 of the 3 authors of the Legacy of the Force series (amongst other books).  It wasn't good.  Only Allston made any attempt to actually keep to the narrative.  The whole thing was a mess with certain characters only seemingly allowed to be written to any degree by specific writers.

Traviss's obsession with clones and Mandalorians and creating a whole culture and language around the latter was always tedious.  The bigging up of Mandalorians generally has been done to a ridiculous degree.  Jedi would have little problem handling them in most circumstance.

Denning was one of those who kept creating more and more over the top "powers" and also seemed to be the one who led to the adoption of "video game" language in the later books.  He couldn't tell you a Jedi or Sith leapt 20 feet; he had to say they "Force-leapt".  He used terms like "Force shield" and "Force telekinesis".  It was bad enough in the narrative passages but including it in character dialogue was utterly jarring.  Could you imagine Obi-Wan saying "Use Force-telekinesis, Luke.  Let go Luke..."? 🤮

author: @SonnetFlambe2767
text:
I've been thinking about that Henry Cavill quote recently in regards to the Devil May Cry Netflix show. So many fans are rightfully upset that the Netflix series blatantly misunderstands what made DMC such a great series in the first place. Instead of being at least somewhat faithful to the games it chose to make a twenty year old franchise into a vessel to tell a completely unrelated political narrative where demons are good all of a sudden and humans (specifically Americans) are the absolute villain.
When Devil May Cry is a series that has always shown that the demons of their universe are EVIL and the very, very few that aren't are explicitly denying their nature to be more HUMAN. "These tears are tears that only humans have." Is quite literally a quote from the first game in the series.
But, for some reason, so many of us fans are being told to just shut up and stop criticizing the series even though we have every right to be upset about how it so heavily mischaracterizes almost EVERYTHING about Devil May Cry. Heroes from the games are treated as villains and murderous, evil villains are treated as creatures made to be sympathized with.
And yet rightful critiques get brushed aside by comments like "you'll just complain about everything!" and claims that you're being "toxic."
Yes, some fans are being a**holes, just like what was mentioned in this video. But most are just genuinely disappointed and want to express that because we're so passionate about what we love. But now the fanbase is divided by strife that should have never existed in the first place had Netflix (or, more specifically, the show's creators) not used the series as a vessel for their own agenda and ideals.
Truly the only good thing to come out of this is the sheer amount of new people playing the games for the first time and discovering how great they are. At the very least we all can be happy about that.

author: @Ghostrunner_GR-74
text:
"X property was always woke" or "X property would be woke if released today" is the defense i hate the most. Because the people saying that statement completely miss the point. They cannot look past the surface level. And they don't even try to.
They say that modern woke movies and shows "tackle" social issues and see that the same properties did so in the past and assume they're both the same, while ignoring the one thing that is the difference. Execution. Whenever social issues were tackled in shows like Dr who or Star Trek they were always done with the idea of making the audience think, not telling them what to think, they did not sacrifice characters or plot to achieve that, and kept an entertainment value.
Woke things today sacrifice everything for their political messaging and their goal is never to make you think. Its to tell you what to think and if you think otherwise you're an ist or a phobe. they don't treat audiences with respect either. there's no subtlety or subtext with anything. Everything is just spoonfed to you, the narrative and the talking points are all spoonfed.  
I am an indian dude but i never once felt that i couldn't relate with the Doctor, or with Spock, or any other character that I liked. But this focus on identity (surface level) over the character and substance is what makes today's shows woke and trash, and shows of that time actually good.

replies.author: @AnonymousNoNayme
replies.text:
It lacks balance/nuance and the reason it is that way is as a result of their extremist ideology. If it had those two key elements (like the objectively superior non-propaganda shows did back in the day) their regressive messaging would be exposed/proven false.

You can't denigrate certain groups and lionize others if you treat everyone equally and actually listen to and steel man their arguments and try to work together towards a mutually beneficial outcome. That would require operating in good faith, giving people the benefit of the doubt and following the golden rule.

That would prove that CRT racism, intersectional feminazi misandry and queer theory bigotry (among various other cultural forms of marxism) is BS and that the leftwing extremist ideologues don't know the first thing about what they are talking.

If you did that you would have to write compelling characters with more than one or two dimensions and interesting stories where cool stuff that matters in the context of the internal lore actually happens.

That would require skill, care and dedication (not to mention self-awareness and personal responsibility) which would allow the wheat to be separated from the chaff naturally based on merit and the market (as opposed to allowing non-creatives and DEI hired activists to dictate what people consume)

If you did that people wouldn't boycott IPs/companies/actors/creatives/execs and the mind-virus couldn't be promulgated to influence the young and/or naive, ignorant or stupid.

Hollywood can't have that! (Justice for Gina Carano. She did nothing wrong.)
